macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Prices Fall Below $71 as Iran Ceasefire Talks Progress
items.summary: Brent crude fell below $71 per barrel amid reports of progress in negotiations to end the US-Israel war on Iran, marking levels unseen since the conflict's start.

items.title: US Supreme Court Strikes Down Campaign Spending Limits in Landmark 6-3 Ruling
items.summary: The Supreme Court invalidated US campaign spending limits citing First Amendment protections, a decision critics warn could amplify wealthy donor influence and special interest sway in politics.

items.title: US Lifts Export Controls on Anthropic's AI Models Fable and Mythos
items.summary: Anthropic announced the restoration of access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 following the US government's removal of export restrictions on its powerful AI models.

items.title: Trump Reports $1.4 Billion in Cryptocurrency Income in Government Filing
items.summary: President Donald Trump disclosed $1.4 billion in crypto income in a new government filing, reinforcing his administration's push for crypto-friendly policies during his second term.

items.title: Bending Spoons Goes Public on Nasdaq with Major Celebration
items.summary: Italian software company Bending Spoons officially listed on the Nasdaq yesterday, with over 400 employees traveling to New York to celebrate the milestone.

items.title: Dow and Nasdaq Drop This Morning Despite Strong June Rally
items.summary: Chip stocks and major indices including the Dow and Nasdaq are down across the board this morning, though the Dow recently hit a new record high wrapping up its best quarter since 2022.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Trump Revealed to Earn $1.4B from Crypto Ventures in First Year Back in Office
items.summary: US President Donald Trump's 2025 financial disclosure reveals he made over $1.4 billion from crypto ventures, including $635 million in royalties from his $TRUMP meme coin and $500 million from World Liberty Financial, while critics note his policies have rolled back Biden-era crypto regulations.

items.title: Trump's $TRUMP Meme Coin Plunges in Value, Leaving Investors Out of Pocket
items.summary: Despite President Trump earning $635 million in royalties from his meme coin, the token has crashed in price since launch, causing significant losses for small investors while the president remains unaffected.

items.title: World Liberty Financial and CIC Digital Tokens Also Plunge After Sales
items.summary: Tokens from World Liberty Financial, a crypto firm founded by Trump's sons, and souvenir meme coins from CIC Digital have both plunged in value since being sold, contributing to the broader decline in Trump-linked crypto assets.

items.title: Trump Rolls Back Biden-Era Crypto Regulations Amid Conflict-of-Interest Scrutiny
items.summary: President Trump has significantly rolled back Biden-era cryptocurrency regulations and placed Paul Atkins, a crypto-friendly official, in charge of the SEC, raising concerns about conflict of interest given his family's $1.4B crypto earnings.

items.title: Arthur Hayes Pardoned by Trump in 2025, Demands Further Crypto Market Deregulation
items.summary: BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes, who received a presidential pardon from Donald Trump in 2025, is now demanding further market deregulation to unleash the full potential of cryptocurrency technology.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Releases Sensor FM: A Trillion-Minute Multimodal Foundation Model for Wearable Health Data
items.summary: Google announced Sensor FM, a massive foundation model trained on over one trillion minutes of multimodal wearable data from 5 million users, enabling 35 clinical health prediction tasks with improved accuracy via an encoder-decoder architecture and virtual agent collaboration.

items.title: ARC-AGI-3 Benchmark Launched: Interactive, Agentic AGI Test That Breaks Current LLMs
items.summary: The new ARC-AGI-3 benchmark makes ARC interactive and agentic, requiring models to discover goals from raw frames rather than transduce static grids; it remains easy for humans but currently breaks all LLMs, with no model achieving 100% yet.

items.title: GLM 5.2 Released from Open Source as Fable and Chad GPT 5.6 Face Government Restrictions
items.summary: GLM 5.2 emerged from an open-source perspective while Fable and Chad GPT 5.6 were banned by government authorities, prompting consumers to hedge bets by owning their AI memory stacks and renting intelligence via tools like Claude and Codex.

items.title: AI Data Center Expansion Drives Unprecedented Memory and Storage Price Surge, Impacting Apple Intelligence Costs
items.summary: The rapid expansion of AI data centers has created an extraordinary surge in demand for memory and storage, leading to unprecedented component price increases that Apple is now passing on to customers for its Apple Intelligence services.

items.title: AI Agents Can Now Build Most of Your Personal AI Memory Stack Using Claude or Codex
items.summary: New agentic AI capabilities allow agents to build most of a user's personal AI memory stack by simply interacting with Claude or Codex, shifting the strategic focus from waiting for big-lab assistants to owning memory and renting intelligence.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Announces End of Physical PlayStation Games Starting January 2028
items.summary: Sony confirmed that all new PlayStation games released from January 2028 onward will be digital-only, effectively ending physical disc production for the platform and impacting all third-party titles including Call of Duty and Battlefield.

items.title: Path of Exile 2's 'Return of the Ancients' Expansion Hits Record Player Peak
items.summary: Just 24 hours ago, Path of Exile 2 reached a record-breaking 124,000 concurrent players during its 'Return of the Ancients' expansion, marking the game's most successful expansion so far despite ongoing technical issues.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals New 'Force of Law' Warbond for Cooperative Shooter
items.summary: Helldivers 2 surprised fans by announcing the upcoming 'Force of Law' Warbond, a new content pack for the cooperative shooter that will soon be available to players.

items.title: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo Announce Nioh 3 During PlayStation State of Play
items.summary: During Sony's June State of Play showcase, Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo officially revealed Nioh 3, a new entry in the soulslike franchise, with immediate availability for fans.

items.title: Former XDefiant Executive Producer Steps Away from Gaming Industry
items.summary: As XDefiant concludes its one-year run, the game's executive producer announced he is stepping away from the gaming industry permanently, marking a significant departure for the franchise.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: X Returns to Brazil After Month-Long Suspension Following Elon Musk Compliance
items.summary: Elon Musk's social media platform X is returning to Brazil after being inaccessible for over a month, having complied with all judicial demands from the Brazilian Supreme Court.

items.title: Amazon Unveils New Delivery Van Technology to Sort Packages on the Fly
items.summary: Amazon has revealed new technology in its delivery vans that will help drivers sort packages during transit, eliminating the need to organize parcels at stops or manually verify deliveries.

items.title: Wimbledon Replaces Line Judges with Electronic Line-Calling Technology
items.summary: The Wimbledon Tennis Tournament is replacing human line judges with electronic line-calling technology, marking a significant shift toward modernization in the sport's oldest Grand Slam event.

items.title: FCC Accelerates Approvals for Next-Generation Satellite Broadband Launches
items.summary: The FCC is moving to create a 'licensing assembly line' to speed up approvals for next-gen satellite broadband, aiming to unleash new satellite systems faster despite concerns about orbital congestion.

items.title: Blue Origin Plans Simplified Infrastructure to Return New Glenn to Flight in 2026
items.summary: Following a May launchpad explosion, Blue Origin is adopting a simpler launch infrastructure to return its New Glenn rocket to flight faster than expected, targeting a 2026 comeback.

items.title: Marriott International Agrees to $52 Million Data Security Settlement
items.summary: Marriott International has agreed to pay $52 million and implement changes to strengthen its data security following a major breach that compromised customer information.

items.title: American Water Cyberattack Highlights Critical Infrastructure Vulnerabilities
items.summary: A continuing cyberattack on American Water, the nation's largest regulated water utility, is renewing focus on the urgent need to protect critical infrastructure from digital threats.

items.title: Bending Spoons Goes Public on Nasdaq with $1.7 Billion IPO
items.summary: Bending Spoons, the owner of AOL, has launched a $1.7 billion IPO on the Nasdaq, marking a major milestone for the tech company after years of private operation.
